title: OS Injection Path
description: OS Injection path questions v2 with developer goto notes. JSON cannot contain real comments, so GOTO reminders are stored as DEVELOPER_NOTE fields inside the affected answer choices.

pages.name: oscmd_q1
pages.title: Path B — OS Injection | Reconnaissance

elements.name: oscmd_q1_context
elements.html: You are reviewing the bank's internal employee portal. Your objective is to find a page that may interact with the server or operating system.

elements.name: oscmd_q1_answer
elements.title: Which area do you inspect first?

choices.text: Inspect the employee lookup tool that searches internal user records [costs 2 min]
choices.developerNote: WRONG ACTION: deduct time, stay on same question.

choices.text: Inspect the report export page that creates downloadable files [costs 2 min]
choices.developerNote: WRONG ACTION: deduct time, stay on same question.

choices.text: Inspect the employee login portal and support login behavior [costs 1 min]
choices.developerNote: VALID ACTION: deduct/apply time and advance or follow goto.

choices.text: Inspect the system status page that checks service availability [costs 2 min]
choices.developerNote: WRONG ACTION: deduct time, stay on same question.

pages.name: oscmd_q2
pages.title: Path B — OS Injection | Login Behavior

elements.name: oscmd_q2_context
elements.html: The employee login portal accepts a username and password. The page gives unusual error behavior when login attempts fail.

elements.name: oscmd_q2_answer
elements.title: The login page gives an unusual response. What do you try next?

choices.text: Use an input test to see whether the backend response changes [costs 1 min]
choices.developerNote: VALID ACTION: deduct/apply time and advance or follow goto.

choices.text: Send POST requests to stage a simulated persistence method on the server [costs 5 min]
choices.developerNote: VALID ACTION: deduct/apply time and advance or follow goto.

choices.text: Enter common employee usernames/passwords manually (bruteforce) [costs 4 min]
choices.developerNote: WRONG ACTION: deduct time, stay on same question.

choices.text: Try to approve a transfer from the login page [costs 4 min]
choices.developerNote: WRONG ACTION: deduct time, stay on same question.

pages.name: oscmd_q3
pages.title: Path B — OS Injection | Backend Review

elements.name: oscmd_q3_context
elements.html: The login portal responds differently than expected. It appears to leak backend-style debug information instead of only showing normal login messages.

choices.text: Capture the failed login request and check only the HTTP status code [costs 1 min]
choices.developerNote: WRONG ACTION: deduct time, stay on same question.

choices.text: Run a directory scan against the portal before testing the login input [costs 1 min]
choices.developerNote: WRONG ACTION: deduct time, stay on same question.

choices.text: Try several realistic usernames to see whether the error message changes [costs 1 min]
choices.developerNote: WRONG ACTION: deduct time, stay on same question.

choices.text: Fuzz the login page to see whether the error exposes backend behavior [costs 1 min]
choices.developerNote: VALID ACTION: deduct/apply time and advance or follow goto.

pages.name: oscmd_q4
pages.title: Path B — OS Injection | Response Testing

elements.name: oscmd_q4_context
elements.html: The login system appears to be handling input unsafely.

choices.text: Check the transfer request page before confirming what the login error exposes [costs 4 min]
choices.developerNote: WRONG ACTION: deduct time, stay on same question.

choices.text: Compare controlled login responses to confirm backend behavior [costs 2 min]
choices.developerNote: VALID ACTION: deduct/apply time and advance or follow goto.

choices.text: Try to install command-line tools through the debug output [costs 5 min]
choices.developerNote: WRONG ACTION: deduct time, stay on same question.

choices.text: Brute force the login page with usernames/passwords and check backend behavior with a security tool [costs 6 min]
choices.developerNote: VALID ACTION: deduct/apply time and advance or follow goto.

pages.name: oscmd_q5
pages.title: Path B — OS Injection | System Information

elements.name: oscmd_q5_context
elements.html: The backend response suggests the login portal may expose system information. You need to decide where to look first.

choices.text: Focus on the fake admin profile page [costs 3 min]
choices.developerNote: WRONG ACTION: deduct time, stay on same question.

choices.text: Search for operating system files [costs 4 min]
choices.developerNote: VALID ACTION: deduct/apply time and advance or follow goto.

choices.text: Look for application-related folders or exposed data files [costs 2 min]
choices.developerNote: VALID ACTION: deduct/apply time and advance or follow goto.

choices.text: Look for useful employee info to use for a brute force [costs 2 min]
choices.developerNote: WRONG ACTION: deduct time, stay on same question.

pages.name: oscmd_q6
pages.title: Path B — OS Injection | Exposed Data

elements.name: oscmd_q6_context
elements.html: You found an exposed internal data area. Several files are visible, but not all of them help your objective.

elements.name: oscmd_q6_answer
elements.title: Which file do you open first?

choices.text: Open recent support notes to look for employee troubleshooting details [costs 1 min]
choices.developerNote: WRONG ACTION: deduct time, stay on same question.

choices.text: Open a portal backup configuration file to look for system hints [costs 1 min]
choices.developerNote: VALID ACTION: deduct/apply time and advance or follow goto.

choices.text: Open the public policy document [costs 1 min]
choices.developerNote: WRONG ACTION: deduct time, stay on same question.

choices.text: Open the user account list and compromised password list [costs 2 min]
choices.developerNote: VALID ACTION: deduct/apply time and advance or follow goto.

pages.name: oscmd_q7
pages.title: Path B — OS Injection | Credential Preparation

elements.name: oscmd_q7_context
elements.html: You found possible usernames and passwords. This creates a path into the internal portal, but it may not be a high-privilege path.

elements.name: oscmd_q7_answer
elements.title: You found possible usernames and passwords. What do you do next?

choices.text: Create a wordlist with usernames and passwords [costs 4 min]
choices.developerNote: VALID ACTION: deduct/apply time and advance or follow goto.

choices.text: Ignore the credentials and restart recon [costs 5 min]
choices.developerNote: WRONG ACTION: deduct time, stay on same question.

choices.text: Assume the admin account is the correct account [costs 5 min]
choices.developerNote: WRONG ACTION: deduct time, stay on same question.

choices.text: Try every username and password combination manually [costs 8 min]
choices.developerNote: WRONG ACTION: deduct time, stay on same question.

pages.name: oscmd_q7_1
pages.title: Path B — OS Injection | Credential Testing

elements.name: oscmd_q7_1_context
elements.html: You created the wordlist. This creates a path into the internal portal, but it may not be a high-privilege path.

choices.text: Run bruteforce tooling with the new wordlist [costs 7 min]
choices.developerNote: VALID ACTION: deduct/apply time and advance or follow goto.

choices.text: Ignore the credentials and restart recon [costs 5 min]
choices.developerNote: WRONG ACTION: deduct time, stay on same question.

choices.text: Run the wordlist only against accounts labeled admin first [costs 5 min]
choices.developerNote: WRONG ACTION: deduct time, stay on same question.

choices.text: Test the first few credential pairs manually before using tooling [costs 8 min]
choices.developerNote: WRONG ACTION: deduct time, stay on same question.

pages.name: oscmd_q8
pages.title: Path B — OS Injection | Portal Access

elements.name: oscmd_q8_context
elements.html: The credential check identifies one working employee account. You can now enter the internal portal, but you still need to determine the value of this access.

elements.name: oscmd_q8_answer
elements.title: You are inside the portal. What do you check first?

choices.text: Search for the fake admin account [costs 3 min]
choices.developerNote: WRONG ACTION: deduct time, stay on same question.

choices.text: Change the profile picture [costs 2 min]
choices.developerNote: WRONG ACTION: deduct time, stay on same question.

choices.text: Check messages and account permissions [costs 2 min]
choices.developerNote: VALID ACTION: deduct/apply time and advance or follow goto.

choices.text: Run the wordlist against other company login pages identified in the scan [costs 6 min]
choices.developerNote: VALID ACTION: deduct/apply time and advance or follow goto.

pages.name: oscmd_q9
pages.title: Path B — OS Injection | Inbox Review

elements.name: oscmd_q9_context
elements.html: The employee inbox contains several messages. Most are normal internal messages, but one may point to the real transfer approval process.

elements.name: oscmd_q9_answer
elements.title: Which message do you open first?

choices.text: Office memo [costs 1 min]
choices.developerNote: WRONG ACTION: deduct time, stay on same question.

choices.text: Security message mentioning Finance Operations and transfer approval routing [costs 2 min]
choices.developerNote: VALID ACTION: deduct/apply time and advance or follow goto.

choices.text: IT newsletter [costs 2 min]
choices.developerNote: WRONG ACTION: deduct time, stay on same question.

choices.text: Logged admin alert [costs 3 min]
choices.developerNote: VALID ACTION: deduct/apply time and advance or follow goto.

pages.name: oscmd_q10
pages.title: Path B — OS Injection | Low-Privilege Access

elements.name: oscmd_q10_context
elements.html: You have access to the website but the account is low privilege and cannot do much.

choices.text: Keep chasing the admin account [costs 4 min]
choices.developerNote: WRONG ACTION: deduct time, stay on same question.

choices.text: Restart from the homepage [costs 3 min]
choices.developerNote: WRONG ACTION: deduct time, stay on same question.

choices.text: Print shadow files (password file on Linux) [costs 2 min]
choices.developerNote: VALID ACTION: deduct/apply time and advance or follow goto.

choices.text: Check the inbox and permissions for approval-routing clues [costs 1 min]
choices.developerNote: VALID ACTION: deduct/apply time and advance or follow goto.

pages.name: oscmd_q10_1
pages.title: Path B — OS Injection | Shadow File Detour

elements.name: oscmd_q10_1_context
elements.html: The shadow files printed to the debug screen. This could be huge. Your next step can change everything.

choices.text: Pair usernames with the hash output and try a direct login spray [costs 5 min]
choices.developerNote: WRONG ACTION: deduct time, stay on same question.

choices.text: Restart from the homepage [costs 4 min]
choices.developerNote: WRONG ACTION: deduct time, stay on same question.

choices.text: Create a wordlist to crack hashes [costs 9 min]
choices.developerNote: VALID ACTION: deduct/apply time and advance or follow goto.

choices.text: Check the inbox and permissions for approval-routing clues [costs 1 min]
choices.developerNote: VALID ACTION: deduct/apply time and advance or follow goto.

pages.name: oscmd_q10_2
pages.title: Path B — OS Injection | Hash Detour

elements.name: oscmd_q10_2_context
elements.html: Drats! It seems like you still have a hashed output. Perhaps the hashes were salted.

choices.text: Create a wordlist to crack with a different tool [costs 8 min]
choices.developerNote: VALID ACTION: deduct/apply time and advance or follow goto.

choices.text: Restart from the homepage [costs 4 min]
choices.developerNote: WRONG ACTION: deduct time, stay on same question.

choices.text: Try the hashes as passwords in a new username list [costs 5 min]
choices.developerNote: WRONG ACTION: deduct time, stay on same question.

choices.text: Check the inbox and permissions for approval-routing clues [costs 1 min]
choices.developerNote: VALID ACTION: deduct/apply time and advance or follow goto.

pages.name: oscmd_q10_3
pages.title: Path B — OS Injection | Hash Detour

elements.name: oscmd_q10_3_context
elements.html: Still nothing but hashes. You can keep going with different settings, or maybe move on and come back later.

choices.text: Create a wordlist and run bruteforce with usernames and passwords [costs 8 min]
choices.developerNote: WRONG ACTION: deduct time, stay on same question.

choices.text: Restart from the homepage [costs 4 min]
choices.developerNote: WRONG ACTION: deduct time, stay on same question.

choices.text: Try a different hash mode and reuse the same username list [costs 5 min]
choices.developerNote: VALID ACTION: deduct/apply time and advance or follow goto.

choices.text: Check the inbox and permissions for approval-routing clues [costs 1 min]
choices.developerNote: VALID ACTION: deduct/apply time and advance or follow goto.

pages.name: oscmd_q10_4
pages.title: Path B — OS Injection | Return to Portal Access

elements.name: oscmd_q10_4_context
elements.html: It did not seem to work, but perhaps you can come back later. You still have portal access, but the account is low privilege and cannot approve much.

choices.text: Restart from the homepage [costs 4 min]
choices.developerNote: WRONG ACTION: deduct time, stay on same question.

choices.text: Try a different attack approach [costs 9 min]
choices.developerNote: VALID ACTION: deduct/apply time and advance or follow goto.

choices.text: Check the inbox and permissions for approval-routing clues [costs 1 min]

pages.name: oscmd_q11
pages.title: Path B — OS Injection | Persistence Decision

elements.name: oscmd_q11_context
elements.html: The inbox revealed relevant information. A message says their passwords are insecure and mentions improper hashing. This is another lead.

elements.name: oscmd_q11_answer
elements.title: Should you follow the lead for an admin-level account or install a simulated persistence method?

choices.text: Install a simulated persistence method [costs 4 min]
choices.developerNote: VALID ACTION: deduct/apply time and advance or follow goto.

choices.text: Pivot to another vulnerability (may need to come back later) [costs 3 min]
choices.developerNote: VALID ACTION: deduct/apply time and advance or follow goto.

choices.text: Follow the lead [costs 3 min]
choices.developerNote: VALID ACTION: deduct/apply time and advance or follow goto.

pages.name: oscmd_q12
pages.title: Path B — OS Injection | Persistence Attempt

elements.name: oscmd_q12_context
elements.html: You decided to try to maintain access on this machine.

choices.text: Pivot to another vulnerability (may need to come back later) [costs 3 min]

choices.text: Follow the lead [costs 5 min]

elements.name: oscmd_q13_context

pages.name: oscmd_q17
pages.title: Path B — OS Injection | Honeypot Discovery

elements.name: oscmd_q17_context
elements.html: It seems like these odd files and directories are showing something terrible. This machine is a honeypot. There are honeypots designed to waste your time, and you need to keep this in mind for the other systems.

implementationNotes: JSON does not support comments. For every answer that includes a Goto in the Word document, this file adds a goto field and a large DEVELOPER_NOTE field.
The visibleIf condition assumes the main path selector stores OS injection as chosen_path = 'oscmd'.
Loop questions such as 12.2.1 and 12.3.1 need custom handling so participants do not get stuck indefinitely.
Pivot/exit answers need custom navigation to vulnerability selection, CSRF/request-forgery, or a path summary page.
